title: Program Manager
description: This position is part of the Office of Management, Department of Energy. As a Program Manager, you will provide direct and responsive support to the immediate offices of the Secretary and Deputy Secretary of Energy, ensuring the seamless provision of a wide array of critical administrative services while developing enterprise-wide policy and strategic frameworks that serve as authoritative models for all DOE components.

title: Information Technology Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: The Networks and Telecommunication Services (NaTS) Service Line is seeking to fill an Information Technology Specialist position. Serves as a senior specialist under the Integrated Network and Infrastructure Office and is responsible for planning, development, installation, operations, and maintenance in support of communications systems and facilities.

title: Information Technology Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: The Networks and Telecommunication Services (NaTS) Service Line is seeking to fill an Information Technology Specialist position. Serves as a senior specialist under the Integrated Network and Infrastructure Office and is responsible for planning, development, installation, operations, and maintenance in support of communications systems and facilities.

As a Grants Management Specialist, you will manage all aspects of the functional area of financial assistance management, including award, solicitation, negotiations, cost and price analysis, award, administration, termination, and close-out for a variety of assistance agreements.

title: Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer/Chief Science and Technology Advisor
description: The Office of Legal Policy (OLP) supports the Attorney General in judicial selection processes and coordinates regulatory review across DOJ components. OLP also advances technical, scientific, and data-informed policy capacity in support of DOJ's mission to uphold the rule of law, protect civil rights, and ensure national security.

title: Attorney Advisor (General)
description: This position is located in the Drug Enforcement Administrator (DEA), Office of Chief Counsel. This position primary purpose is to provide legal advice and support to DEA management and field offices worldwide. CC attorneys coordinate with DEA investigative personnel, provide legal training, and respond to requests for assistance from DEA Division Counsel. Department of Justice (DOJ) agencies post experienced attorney job announcements on the DOJ Careers page.

title: Operations Research Analyst (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as an Operations Research Analyst in the Master Planning and Integration Branch, within the Spaceport Development Division. Provide support development of a centralized spaceport model, integrate technical requirements into planning, modeling, and analysis, while tracking impacts to spaceport common use infrastructure. Evaluate operations impacts from increased launch activity and help identify operational risks associated with future missions and help inform future spaceport investments.

title: Operations Research Analyst (Direct Hire)
description: As the Modeling and Analysis Technical SME in the Master Planning and Integration Branch, supporting the Spaceport Development Division, you will support development of a centralized spaceport model, integrate technical requirements into planning, modeling, and analysis, while tracking impacts to spaceport infrastructure. You will evaluate operations impacts from increased launch activity and identify operational risks associated with future missions and help inform future spaceport investments.

title: Information Technology Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: As an Information Technology Specialist within the Workplace & Collaboration Services (WCS) service line, part of the Operations Division within NASA OCIO, you will be responsible for providing customer-centric, innovative, efficient, and evolving IT services to enhance the productivity and collaboration of the NASA workforce. WCS offers collaboration, computer, mobile device, print, and helpdesk capabilities, as well as identity, credential, and access management services.

title: Management and Program Analyst (Direct Hire)
description: As a Management and Program Analyst in the Office of Management, you will be responsible for policies, procedures, resources, procurement and facilities, assisting in evaluating the effectiveness of the programs, processes, and operations.

title: Emergency Management Specialist (Direct Hire)
locations: White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico
description: The position oversees the NASA White Sands Test Facility Fire Department and Emergency Dispatch Center to ensure the appropriate fire protection and response capability exists. You will lead and support NASA's emergency management and fire mission by developing plans, training, and readiness activities to protect personnel, facilities, and critical operations in order to prevent, respond to, and recover from hazards; strengthening continuity, safety, and mission resilience across NASA programs.

title: Emergency Management Specialist (Direct Hire)
locations: White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico
description: The position oversees the NASA White Sands Test Facility Fire Department and Emergency Dispatch Center to ensure the appropriate fire protection and response capability exists. title: Security Specialist (Direct Hire)

You will work with a team of highly skilled Security Specialists, interact daily with internal and external stakeholders while assisting with or leading physical security related initiatives as outlined in agency and federal policies.

title: Security Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: As a Security Specialist with NASA's Office of Protective Service, you will be responsible for assisting in the overall management and administration of the Physical Security Program. You will manage the internal OPO certification of flight readiness process for the as-built spacecraft and perform assessments of spacecraft technical, and schedule risk and AI&P performance.

This announcement will close at 11:59 p.m. ET on the date the first 50 applications are received or 05/26/2026, whichever comes first.

title: Aerospace Engineer, AST, Research Pilot (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as an Aerospace Engineer/Research Pilot in the Research Services Directorate which involves the piloting of aircraft (and/or Uninhabited Aerial Systems(UASs)) used in research, development, and test programs; planning/evaluating test parameters; performing flight and/or ground simulations of advanced missions and aircraft; participating with project engineers and scientists responsible for development, design, testing, evaluation, and reporting on new aircraft or aircraft systems.

title: Business Enterprise Analyst
description: Reemployed annuitant: This vacancy does not meet the criteria for appointment of annuitants. This job announcement uses the USA Hire Assessment to evaluate applicants. For more information, visit https://support-usahire.opm.gov/hc/en-us.

title: Supervisory General Attorney (Labor)
description: This position is located in the Department of Labor, Office of the Solicitor (SOL), Division of Management and Administrative Legal Services (MALS). We are seeking a FOIA and Info Law attorney to serve as DOL's senior advisor on legal issues related to the Freedom of Information Act, Privacy Act, and other related information law statutes and the protection and handling of personal, private, controlled unclassified, or proprietary information-with the potential to damage the public's trust.

title: Direct Hire Public Notice - Data Scientist
description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) will use Direct Hire Authority to fill vacancies for the Data Scientist positions within the agency. This vacancy is a REPOSITORY of applications. Applicants MAY BE periodically referred to Selecting Officials both during the open period and for up to 90 days after the closing date of the vacancy. Because of the large number of applications anticipated, applicants status will not be updated UNLESS referred.

title: Trial Attorney (Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation)
description: The Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation Branch seeks Trial Attorneys to oversee high-impact affirmative litigation that ensures nationwide compliance with federal law. The Branch offers a frontline role in the Department's major civil enforcement initiatives. Well-qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $25,000, with priority given to applicants in D.C., Raleigh, San Francisco, Dallas, and N.Y.C. However, strong applicants from any location are encouraged to apply.

title: Trial Attorney (Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation)
description: The Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation Branch seeks Trial Attorneys to oversee high-impact affirmative litigation that ensures nationwide compliance with federal law. The Branch offers a frontline role in the Department's major civil enforcement initiatives. Well-qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $25,000 depending on qualifications, education, and other factors. All recipients of a signing bonus are required to fulfill a service obligation.

title: Marine Interdiction Agent
description: This position is covered by Law Enforcement Availability Pay (LEAP). LEAP is an additional compensation of 25% of the base salary and locality pay. See additional pay information in the Duties section. Organizational Location: This position is with the Department of Homeland Security, within U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Air and Marine Operations.

title: Director, Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology
organization: Office of the Secretary of Transportation
description: This position leads and oversees the Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology within the Department of Transportation's Office of the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology (OST-R), John A. Volpe National Transportation Center (Volpe), located in Cambridge, MA. The position is comprised of seven technical divisions with a combined federal staffing of more than 103 employees, a portfolio of 166 projects, and annual obligations of over $81million, on average.

title: Foreign Service Office Management Specialist (FS 9017)
locations: Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago
District of Columbia, District of Columbia
description: The U.S. Department of State is developing a rank-ordered list of eligible hires for a limited number of Foreign Service Office Management Specialist (OMS) positions. Learn more about Foreign Service careers at https://careers.state.gov. There is more to joining the Foreign Service than just the base salary. Refer to the Benefits section for more information on total compensation.

title: IT Specialist
description: As an Information Technology (IT) Specialist in the Federal government, you will perform duties related to the installation and configuration of computer hardware, software, operating systems, networks, applications, and printers and scanners; providing technical support and troubleshooting issues; assisting with the maintenance of computer systems and networks; and ensuring the application of appropriate IT security measures.
